summary_en: FY3/27 annual dividend raised to ¥46/share (interim and year-end each ¥23, +¥3 vs prior forecast). Consolidated payout-ratio floor lifted from 30%+ to 50%+ under medium-term plan 'JCM Global Vision 2032 – Next Growth Stage'.
title_en: Notice Regarding Change in Dividend Policy and Revision of Dividend Forecast for Fiscal Year Ending March 2027 (Dividend Increase)
translation_en_brief: Japan Cash Machine Co., Ltd. (TSE Prime: 6418) announced on 21 May 2026 a revision of its dividend policy and a hike to its FY3/27 dividend forecast. The board resolved to raise the annual dividend to ¥46 per share, with interim and year-end dividends each set at ¥23 (an increase of ¥3 from the prior forecast of ¥20 each). Concurrently, the company shifted its consolidated payout-ratio target from a floor of 30% to a floor of 50%, effective immediately, to deepen shareholder returns while pursuing earnings growth under its new medium-term plan 'JCM Global Vision 2032 – Next Growth Stage' (through March 2029). The prior-year dividend (FY3/26) was ¥40 per share (¥20 interim, ¥20 year-end).
